summary: Crystal‑clear Charlie Miller soundboard captures an energetic Shoreline ’90 set with epic Playing In The Band > Eyes of the World, soaring Stella Blue and great Birdsong, though the band swings up‑and‑down at times.
review: The recording is a superbly remastered soundboard mix that showcases Jerry’s midi wizardry and a tight rhythm section. The show delivers several high points – a marathon Playing In The Band into Eyes of the World, an ecstatic Stella Blue solo, classic Iko Iko with Brent on vocals, and a rousing Weight encore – but some listeners note uneven moments early in the second set.

ai_rating.rationale: Recording quality is consistently praised as excellent; show performance has both strong, memorable peaks and noted up‑and‑down sections, leading to an overall rating just under 4 stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, well‑balanced board mix; Charlie Miller’s remaster praised for crispness and lack of dropouts or muddiness.

standout_songs: Playing In The Band > Eyes of the World
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds solid energy; second set is uneven at the start but clicks after the drums, culminating in a soaring Stella Blue and an epic jam that lifts the entire performance.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His midi‑generated patches are dialed in, with hot leads on Rooster, vivid textures in Space and Lovelight, and an ecstatic final solo on Stella Blue.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboard work shines, especially singing on Iko Iko; overall keyboards provide spacy flutes and falling‑star effects that lift the jams.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drumming provides a turning point in the second set – once the drums come in, the show “clicks” and energy rises.
